- Backlog Item ID: BI-260016
- Title: Formalize stack selection criteria and concrete MVP stack
- Persona/Actor: Frontend implementation team and domain operators
- Value statement: For predictable delivery of graph-first workflows, establish explicit stack criteria and a concrete MVP stack that aligns with accepted architecture constraints.
- Context: Stack selection decision phase for H1-H9.
- Problem statement: Without explicit stack criteria and a concrete decision, implementation can drift into mismatched tools and unstable architecture.
- Expected outcome: Team can implement with one approved stack baseline and clear acceptance/rejection criteria.
- Success metric(s):
  - H1-H9 are accepted and encoded in formal artifacts.
  - FR/OR/CR requirements define concrete stack behavior, operations, and constraints.
- Acceptance criteria (behavior):
  - ADR-260019 is created and accepted.
  - REQ-FR-260017, REQ-FR-260018, REQ-OR-260004, and REQ-CR-260008 are created and linked.
  - Product backlog and backlog status include BI-260016 with traceability.
- Non-goals: Multi-runtime deployment split in MVP, production auth-provider integration in MVP, and stack experimentation outside accepted criteria.
- Constraints: Preserve accepted G architecture boundaries and prior C-F UX/IA decisions.
- Dependencies: Accepted A-G decisions and backend GraphQL contract decisions.
- Risks/assumptions:
  - Risk: dependency/version churn can pressure baseline stability.
  - Risk: insufficient enforcement may allow rejected stack patterns to reappear.
  - Assumption: selected baseline remains adequate through MVP validation cycle.
- Validation approach: stack review checks, architecture/CI checks, and core-workflow execution checks.
- Priority score: 90
- Target window: MVP foundation
- Links:
  - Requirement entry: REQ-FR-260017.md, REQ-FR-260018.md, REQ-OR-260004.md, REQ-CR-260008.md
  - ADR: ADR-260019.md
  - ICR: n/a
  - Changeset: n/a
